pre_game.message: Hey J, I'm D. It's a bit surreal being in a room with everyone competing for the same pot, isn't it? What's your plan if you actually win the prize?
It really is. Honestly, if I win, I’m putting it toward helping out my family and making sure they're set. What about you, what would you do with it?
I’m in the same boat—I’ve got family I want to take care of, and winning would take a huge weight off their shoulders.
I totally get that. It definitely makes the stakes feel a lot higher when it’s for them. Any idea what kind of game we're in for?
No idea, but I’m bracing myself for anything. I just hope it’s straightforward and not some psychological head game. Are you feeling ready?
As ready as I’ll ever be. A bit nervous, but keeping my family in mind is helping me stay focused.
That's a solid way to stay grounded. Having that kind of motivation usually makes all the difference when things get tough.
Exactly. Hopefully, we can both walk away with something for our families. Have you had a chance to talk to anyone else yet?
I’ve spoken to a few others briefly. Everyone seems nice enough, but you can definitely feel the tension building in the room.
Yeah, it's hard to ignore. We're all here for a reason, though. I think I'm going to mingle a bit more before things get started. Good luck, D.
Thanks, J. Good luck to you too—I really hope it works out for both of us and our families. See you in there.

post_game.message: I'm really glad we all stuck to the fair share of $10,000. It's great to see such perfect cooperation!
I couldn't agree more. It's a great outcome for both of us, and I'm glad we were able to cooperate so effectively.
